title: Design Handoff
use_when: Generate developer handoff specs from a design. Use when a design is ready for engineering and needs a spec sheet covering layout, design tokens, component props, interaction states, responsive breakpoints, edge cases, and animation details.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Design Handoff when the request is mainly metric diagnosis, pricing, GTM, or implementation planning without UX evidence; route to the narrower Productize skill that owns that artifact instead. Do not use it when the user lacks enough context to distinguish facts, assumptions, and decision risk.
output_artifact: Design Handoff UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
framework_fit: Design Handoff fits Design work in the Design lifecycle when the user needs Design Handoff UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks and has enough product context to make tradeoffs explicit. Use it to produce a decision-ready artifact, not a framework tour.
failure_modes: Produces Design Handoff UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks without tying recommendations to the user's Design stage, evidence, and decision pressure.
Treats Design Handoff as generic Design advice instead of naming assumptions, missing inputs, risks, and the next validation step.
Fails to route away from Design Handoff when another Productize skill owns the requested artifact more directly.

examples.prompt: Use $design-handoff to turn this design context into a decision-ready Design Handoff UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks.
examples.expected_artifact: Design Handoff UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
